paper_title: Multivariate Analysis of Lower Cretaceous Monosulcate Pollen from Central-Western Argentina

methods.description: This method involves analyzing fossil and extant monosulcate pollen grains to detect morphological affinities and variations using multivariate statistical techniques.

steps.description: We analyzed a total of 639 fossil specimens from the six selected palynological assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fossil specimens from palynological assemblages

steps.description: We discarded damaged or folded specimens and they were not considered in the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out damaged or folded specimens

steps.description: We added published data from in-situ cycadalean pollen of three Androstrobus species to our fossil dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: add published cycadalean pollen data to dataset

steps.description: We examined an extant monosulcate pollen grain dataset and registered the same morphological variables that we previously observed on fossil specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: examine extant pollen dataset and register morphological variables

steps.description: We performed a cluster and discriminant analyses to detect their morphological affinities.
We coded a set of morphological variables observed in our fossil pollen grains.
We performed a principal component analysis with seven morphological features to obtain an integrated response from the variables.
We made a cluster analysis with the complete set of variables to divide the set of fossil data into two subsets according to their similarities.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis to divide data into subsets

steps.description: We performed an analysis of variance (ANOVA) using the continuous values of polar and equatorial diameters to support the cluster differentiation.
We conducted a discriminant analysis to observe the separation of groups.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct discriminant analysis for group separation

steps.description: We added the extant monosulcate pollen grain dataset to our analysis and used the most relevant features to analyze a combined dataset of fossils and extant specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze combined dataset of fossils and extant specimens

steps.description: We performed the data analyses and created the graphs using R version 4.0.
